name: Hobblebush
summary: The single most diagnostic shrub of the Northern Hardwood Forest reference community — explicitly named in PA DCNR / PNHP community descriptions but missing from most restoration plantings. Sprawling, layering shrub of cool moist understory under sugar maple and yellow birch.
notes: Distinctive low-arching branches that root where they touch the ground (the 'hobble' habit). Pre-leaf white flower clusters in May; red berries ripen to purple-black in August for thrushes. Viburnum genus supports ~101 Lepidoptera species (Spring Azure, Cecropia, Hummingbird Clearwing, Henry's Elfin). Plant on north-facing slopes, in cool moist coves, under existing yellow birch or hemlock canopy.

planting.soil: Rich, moist, acidic to neutral; deep duff layer
planting.site_notes: Plant under existing canopy on north-facing slopes or in cool coves — will not thrive in open sun or dry sites. The layering habit means a single plant becomes a thicket over time. Wire cage essential; heavily browsed by deer.
